societyTypes.name: Forager / ritual-tribal
societyTypes.shortDescription: Mobile or semi-settled society with oral tradition, limited formal hierarchy, and little bureaucracy.

societyTypes.name: Pastoral clan / nomadic warrior
societyTypes.shortDescription: Kin-based, mobile, animal-centered society with practical martial and survival training.

societyTypes.name: Early agrarian chiefdom / proto-state
societyTypes.shortDescription: Settled farming society with local hierarchy and growing specialization.

societyTypes.name: Bronze Age palace state
societyTypes.shortDescription: Palace-centered society with elite coordination, recordkeeping, and specialized craft.
societyTypes.notes: Use as palace-state variant of the source row

societyTypes.name: Temple-state / early literate agrarian state
societyTypes.shortDescription: Early literate state with priesthood, scribal culture, taxation, and formal ritual order.
societyTypes.notes: Literate temple-state interpretation of source row

societyTypes.name: Imperial agrarian bureaucracy
societyTypes.shortDescription: Large organized state with formal offices, recordkeeping, and infrastructure.

societyTypes.name: Feudal / manorial kingdom
societyTypes.shortDescription: Rank-based landholding society with noble households, retainers, and local obligations.

societyTypes.name: Classical polis / city-state civilization
societyTypes.shortDescription: Urban civic society with rhetoric, education, public life, and structured elite culture.

societyTypes.name: Imperial classical / Hellenistic-Roman high civilization
societyTypes.shortDescription: Highly organized urban imperial culture with law, engineering, and broad learned life.

societyTypes.name: Court-bureaucratic high medieval empire
societyTypes.shortDescription: Literate imperial court culture with ceremony, bureaucracy, and high-status administration.
societyTypes.notes: Court-bureaucratic interpretation of source civilization row

societyTypes.name: Commercial renaissance city-state / principality
societyTypes.shortDescription: Urban commercial elite culture with diplomacy, finance, advanced technique, and refined court life.

taxonomyGroups.name: Animal Handling
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Animal care, training, riding, and related work.

taxonomyGroups.name: Athletics
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Movement, balance, climbing, swimming, and body control.

taxonomyGroups.name: Humanities
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Literacy, language, history, theology, law, and related learned culture.

taxonomyGroups.name: Medicine
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Healing, remedies, surgery, and related care.

taxonomyGroups.name: Operations
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Trade, administration, accounting, and practical organization.

taxonomyGroups.name: Performance
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Expressive, staged, musical, and spoken performance.

taxonomyGroups.name: Physical Science
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Measurement, mathematics, astronomy, and related inquiry.

taxonomyGroups.name: Security
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Search, locks, traps, concealment, and related covert handling.

taxonomyGroups.name: Combat
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Attack, defense, weapon-family skills, and combat styles.

taxonomyGroups.name: Military
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Leadership, battlefield skill, veteran nerve, and unit fighting.

taxonomyGroups.name: Craft
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Pottery, weaving, carpentry, smithing, and related making skills.

taxonomyGroups.name: Mental
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Concentration, memory, self-control, meditation, and awareness.

taxonomyGroups.name: Mystical
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Divination, omen reading, ritual interpretation, and astrology.

taxonomyGroups.name: Wilderness
taxonomyGroups.shortDescription: Seamanship, navigation, riding, animal handling, and outdoor survival-adjacent practice.

trainingGroups.name: Literate Foundation
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Basic literate education for scribes, scholars, clerks, and formal learners.

trainingGroups.name: Civic Learning
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Formal literate office, law, and rhetorical culture.

trainingGroups.name: Sacred Learning
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Priestly, doctrinal, and ritual-intellectual training.

trainingGroups.name: Mercantile Practice
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical trade, bargaining, and valuation training.

trainingGroups.name: Commercial Administration
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Record-heavy office and mercantile administration training.

trainingGroups.name: Courtly Formation
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Basic training in elite manners, rank, and ceremonial conduct.

trainingGroups.name: Political Acumen
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Reading people, factions, and elite social situations.
trainingGroups.notes: Source group: Empathy / Etiquette / Operations overlap.

trainingGroups.name: Healing Practice
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical organized healing and immediate care.

trainingGroups.name: Herb and Remedy Craft
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Plant, remedy, and poison-related healing knowledge.

trainingGroups.name: Maritime Crew Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Basic sailor and working seamanship training.

trainingGroups.name: Maritime Navigation
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Advanced maritime route-finding.

trainingGroups.name: Animal Husbandry
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical stock, herd, and animal-working training.

trainingGroups.name: Mounted Service
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Riding and mounted practical service training.

trainingGroups.name: Transport and Caravan Work
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Overland freight and movement.

trainingGroups.name: Covert Entry
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Burglary, stealth entry, and covert access skills.

trainingGroups.name: Street Theft
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Urban theft and covert carry.
trainingGroups.notes: Source group: Sleight of Hand / Stealth overlap.

trainingGroups.name: Trap and Intrusion Work
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Technical intrusion and trap-handling.

trainingGroups.name: Fieldcraft Stealth
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Rural stealth, concealment, and ambush skills.

trainingGroups.name: Athletic Conditioning
trainingGroups.shortDescription: General movement and body-control training.

trainingGroups.name: Social Reading
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Interpersonal judgment and emotional awareness.

trainingGroups.name: Performance Basics
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Broad entertainer and expressive performance package.

trainingGroups.name: Formal Performance
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Elite, staged, or formal speaking/performance training.

trainingGroups.name: Mental Discipline
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Trained inner control and focus.
trainingGroups.notes: Formation group, not always a default profession core. Source group: Meta-Cognition.

trainingGroups.name: Technical Measurement
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical technical, surveying, and measurement training.
trainingGroups.notes: Source group: Engineering / Physical Science overlap.

trainingGroups.name: Learned Natural Inquiry
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Advanced scientific and scholarly investigation.

trainingGroups.name: Omen and Ritual Practice
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical divinatory and ritual-interpretive training.

trainingGroups.name: Basic Melee Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: One melee attack skill plus Dodge and Parry.
trainingGroups.notes: Choose one melee attack skill in addition to the listed skills.

trainingGroups.name: Advanced Melee Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Two related melee attack skills plus Dodge and Parry.
trainingGroups.notes: Choose two related melee attack skills in addition to the listed skills.

trainingGroups.name: Basic Missile Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: One missile attack skill plus Dodge and Perception.
trainingGroups.notes: Choose one missile attack skill in addition to the listed skills.

trainingGroups.name: Advanced Missile Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Broader trained ranged-combat package.
trainingGroups.notes: Choose one main missile skill and one secondary missile or fallback skill in addition to the listed skills.

trainingGroups.name: Defensive Soldiering
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Defensive discipline and line-fighting package.

trainingGroups.name: Mounted Warrior Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Proper mounted combat package.

trainingGroups.name: Field Soldiering
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Practical field-combat adaptation package.
trainingGroups.notes: Choose one attack skill in addition to the listed skills.

trainingGroups.name: Veteran Soldiering
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Hardened battlefield survival and veteran composure package.

trainingGroups.name: Officer Training
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Battlefield leadership and control package.

trainingGroups.name: Veteran Leadership
trainingGroups.shortDescription: Seasoned military command under pressure.

skills.name: Literacy
skills.shortDescription: Read and write a script used by the character’s culture.
skills.contextNotes: Gateway skill for learned and administrative roles.

skills.name: Language
skills.shortDescription: Speak and understand a language.
skills.contextNotes: Spoken language is universal; written use depends on Literacy.

skills.name: Specific Language
skills.shortDescription: Mastery of one specific language.
skills.contextNotes: Best for scholars, diplomats, merchants, and elite specialists.

skills.name: History
skills.shortDescription: Know remembered past events, lineages, places, and major developments.
skills.contextNotes: Exists orally and textually; becomes broader in literate societies.

skills.name: Theology
skills.shortDescription: Know religious belief, doctrine, rites, and sacred tradition.
skills.contextNotes: More formal in organized sacred cultures.

skills.name: Philosophy
skills.shortDescription: Use structured reasoning about ethics, logic, metaphysics, and knowledge.

skills.name: Law
skills.shortDescription: Know legal rules, disputes, property, obligations, and judgment.
skills.contextNotes: Broad legal-cultural skill; could later split customary vs codified law.

skills.name: Bureaucratic Writing
skills.shortDescription: Write formal records, reports, decrees, petitions, and official correspondence.
skills.contextNotes: Narrower than full literate culture; ideal half-cost support skill.

skills.name: Rhetorical Composition
skills.shortDescription: Compose persuasive, formal, or elegant argument.

skills.name: Measurement
skills.shortDescription: Apply length, area, weight, volume, and proportion in practical use.

skills.name: Mathematics
skills.shortDescription: Use number, proportion, calculation, and quantitative reasoning.

skills.name: Astronomy
skills.shortDescription: Understand celestial movement for calendar, orientation, or study.

skills.name: Alchemy
skills.shortDescription: Work with substances, transformations, mixtures, and proto-chemical theory.

skills.name: Natural Philosophy
skills.shortDescription: Reason about nature, matter, and the physical world.
skills.contextNotes: Better as an elite support skill than a base profession skill.

skills.name: Surveying
skills.shortDescription: Measure land, boundaries, gradients, and layout in the field.
skills.contextNotes: Practical land-measurement and site-layout skill for builders, engineers, roads, estates, and fortifications.

skills.name: Mechanics
skills.shortDescription: Understand simple machines, force transfer, moving parts, and practical mechanisms.
skills.contextNotes: Applied technical skill for devices, structural motion, simple machines, and engineering problem-solving.

skills.name: Bargaining
skills.shortDescription: Negotiate prices, terms, favors, and exchanges.

skills.name: Gambling
skills.shortDescription: Understand games of chance, betting behavior, and risk-taking.

skills.name: Trading
skills.shortDescription: Manage regular exchange of goods across households, markets, or routes.

skills.name: Banking
skills.shortDescription: Handle credit, deposits, transfers, loans, and financial instruments.

skills.name: Administration
skills.shortDescription: Run offices, records, logistics, and institutional routine.
skills.contextNotes: Core official, steward, and governance skill.

skills.name: Intrigue
skills.shortDescription: Work within covert politics, factions, patronage, and hidden agendas.

skills.name: Bookkeeping
skills.shortDescription: Keep structured records of quantities, payments, inventories, and obligations.

skills.name: Appraisal
skills.shortDescription: Estimate quality, authenticity, condition, and value of goods.
skills.contextNotes: Valuable merchant and rogue support skill.

skills.name: Etiquette
skills.shortDescription: Know expected manners, forms of respect, and socially correct behavior.

skills.name: Etiquette by Culture
skills.shortDescription: Apply etiquette within one specific culture or court tradition.

skills.name: Heraldry
skills.shortDescription: Recognize arms, badges, lineages, and formal symbols of status.
skills.contextNotes: Distinct noble and military identity skill.

skills.name: Courtly Protocol
skills.shortDescription: Manage precedence, audiences, titles, and ceremonial court behavior.
skills.contextNotes: Narrow but strong elite support skill.

skills.name: Insight
skills.shortDescription: Read mood, intention, sincerity, fear, uncertainty, or emotional state.

skills.name: Social Perception
skills.shortDescription: Notice status cues, alliances, tension, and interpersonal dynamics in groups.

skills.name: Detect Lies
skills.shortDescription: Judge whether speech or behavior suggests deception or concealment.

skills.name: Seduction
skills.shortDescription: Influence attraction or intimacy through timing, attention, and emotional reading.
skills.contextNotes: Attractive but niche social support skill.

skills.name: Singing
skills.shortDescription: Use the voice for song, pitch, rhythm, and expression.

skills.name: Dancing
skills.shortDescription: Perform structured or improvised movement for ritual, celebration, or display.

skills.name: Storytelling
skills.shortDescription: Hold attention through spoken narrative, pacing, memory, and dramatic delivery.

skills.name: Music
skills.shortDescription: Play or perform structured instrumental music.

skills.name: Acting
skills.shortDescription: Adopt role, gesture, emotion, and presentation for staged or social performance.
skills.contextNotes: Strong performer and infiltrator support skill.

skills.name: Recitation
skills.shortDescription: Deliver memorized text, verse, doctrine, or formal speech with control.

skills.name: Oratory
skills.shortDescription: Deliver persuasive public speech to assemblies, courts, or elites.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Social skill; source stats Int / Cha.

skills.name: Concentration
skills.shortDescription: Sustain attention and mental focus under distraction or pressure.

skills.name: Memory
skills.shortDescription: Retain, recall, and organize learned details or patterns.

skills.name: Self-Control
skills.shortDescription: Govern impulse, fear, pain response, and emotional reaction under pressure.
skills.contextNotes: Broad mental and emotional control core.

skills.name: Meditation
skills.shortDescription: Use disciplined stillness, breath, or contemplation to regulate inner state.

skills.name: Perception
skills.shortDescription: Notice relevant details, threats, movement, and hidden information.
skills.contextNotes: Core observation skill in and out of combat.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Action skill; source stats Int / Pow; note: 'no more luck'.

skills.name: Animal care
skills.shortDescription: Feed, calm, house, and maintain animals in routine condition.

skills.name: Animal Training
skills.shortDescription: Train animals to obey commands or perform useful tasks.

skills.name: Herding
skills.shortDescription: Manage grazing animals and control flock or herd behavior.

skills.name: Riding
skills.shortDescription: Ride and control a trained mount in travel, stress, or basic action.

skills.name: Teamstering
skills.shortDescription: Handle draft animals and direct wagons, carts, teams, or pack trains.

skills.name: Falconry
skills.shortDescription: Keep, train, and work birds of prey for hunting, display, or status use.

skills.name: Sailing
skills.shortDescription: Handle sails and work aboard a vessel under routine maritime conditions.

skills.name: Navigation
skills.shortDescription: Determine route, direction, and position at sea or on major waters.

skills.name: Navigation by Environment
skills.shortDescription: Navigate one environment such as open sea, coasts, rivers, lakes, or ice waters.

skills.name: Ropework
skills.shortDescription: Tie, maintain, and use lines, knots, and rigging.

skills.name: Boat Handling
skills.shortDescription: Maneuver small craft or directly control a vessel in tight or rough conditions.

skills.name: Climb
skills.shortDescription: Climb natural or built surfaces safely and efficiently.

skills.name: Run
skills.shortDescription: Sprint, pace, pursue, and move rapidly on foot.

skills.name: Jump
skills.shortDescription: Leap gaps, obstacles, and vertical distances with control.

skills.name: Swim
skills.shortDescription: Move safely and effectively through water.
skills.contextNotes: Broad physical survival and adventure core.

skills.name: Balance
skills.shortDescription: Keep footing on narrow, unstable, moving, or slippery surfaces.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.shortDescription: Tumble, roll, control falls, and use advanced body control.
skills.contextNotes: Stylish secondary; should stay narrower than core athletics.

skills.name: Search
skills.shortDescription: Search persons, containers, rooms, or sites for hidden items or traces.
skills.contextNotes: Broad enough to support several roles.

skills.name: Conceal Object
skills.shortDescription: Hide small items on the body, in baggage, or in surroundings.

skills.name: Trap Handling
skills.shortDescription: Detect, avoid, disarm, or set traps and trigger mechanisms.

skills.name: Pick lock
skills.shortDescription: Open locks without the proper key using tools and tactile knowledge.

skills.name: Pickpocket
skills.shortDescription: Steal small items from a person without immediate detection.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.shortDescription: Move quietly, stay unnoticed, and avoid attention while approaching or withdrawing.

skills.name: Hide
skills.shortDescription: Use cover, terrain, darkness, or clutter to remain unseen.

skills.name: Camouflage
skills.shortDescription: Blend clothing, gear, and body into terrain or surroundings.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.shortDescription: Alter appearance, manner, and presentation to pass as another type of person.

skills.name: Pottery
skills.shortDescription: Shape, fire, and finish clay vessels and ceramic goods.

skills.name: Weaving
skills.shortDescription: Produce woven cloth, bands, and simple textiles.

skills.name: Leatherworking
skills.shortDescription: Cut, cure, shape, and repair leather goods and hide equipment.

skills.name: Carpentry
skills.shortDescription: Make and repair wooden structures, fittings, tools, and objects.

skills.name: Tailoring
skills.shortDescription: Cut, fit, alter, and finish garments.

skills.name: Baking
skills.shortDescription: Prepare and bake bread and staple oven foods reliably.

skills.name: Brewing
skills.shortDescription: Produce fermented drink from grain, fruit, honey, or similar materials.

skills.name: Stoneworking
skills.shortDescription: Cut, dress, shape, and fit stone for tools, structures, and finishes.

skills.name: Smithing
skills.shortDescription: Forge, shape, and repair metal tools, fittings, and simple equipment.

skills.name: Blacksmith
skills.shortDescription: Forge and repair general metal tools and common metalwork.

skills.name: Armorer
skills.shortDescription: Make and repair armor and defensive equipment.

skills.name: Weapon Smith
skills.shortDescription: Make and repair melee weapons and related combat metalwork.

skills.name: Weapon Maintenance
skills.shortDescription: Maintain, repair, and keep weapons serviceable in field or garrison use.
skills.contextNotes: Support skill, not front-line combat skill.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.shortDescription: Diagnose and treat disease, injury, and bodily disorders using organized medical practice.
skills.contextNotes: Core physician and healer profession skill.

skills.name: First aid
skills.shortDescription: Give immediate practical treatment to stabilize wounds, bleeding, shock, and urgent injury.

skills.name: Herb lore
skills.shortDescription: Know medicinal, harmful, and useful plants and their common uses.
skills.contextNotes: Strong low-tech or hybrid healing core.

skills.name: Poison Lore
skills.shortDescription: Know poisons, toxins, symptoms, preparation, and antidotal principles.
skills.contextNotes: Broad enough to define roles on its own.

skills.name: Pharmacy
skills.shortDescription: Prepare, compound, preserve, and dispense remedies and medical substances.

skills.name: Nursing
skills.shortDescription: Give ongoing care, monitoring, hygiene, and recovery support.

skills.name: Surgery
skills.shortDescription: Treat wounds and bodily problems through invasive intervention.

skills.name: Bone Setting
skills.shortDescription: Set fractures, dislocations, and structural injuries.

skills.name: Obstetrics
skills.shortDescription: Manage pregnancy, childbirth, and maternal care.

skills.name: Poison Treatment
skills.shortDescription: Diagnose and treat poisoning, envenomation, and toxic injury.

skills.name: Battlefield Surgery
skills.shortDescription: Perform urgent surgical treatment under combat or camp conditions.
skills.contextNotes: Common-professional or elite depending later tuning.

skills.name: Heart Surgery
skills.shortDescription: Perform highly advanced thoracic or cardiac surgery.

skills.name: Divination
skills.shortDescription: Interpret signs, omens, visions, or ritual indicators for hidden meaning.

skills.name: Omen Reading
skills.shortDescription: Read natural signs, chance events, or behavior as meaningful omens.

skills.name: Ritual Interpretation
skills.shortDescription: Interpret ritual forms, sacred procedures, and prescribed signs.

skills.name: Astrology
skills.shortDescription: Use heavenly bodies and sign systems to interpret destiny, timing, or influence.

skills.name: Augury
skills.shortDescription: Interpret omens through a formalized sanctioned method.

skills.name: Dodge
skills.shortDescription: Avoid attacks through movement, timing, and body positioning.
skills.contextNotes: Core defense; part of real melee training.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Parry
skills.shortDescription: Defend with weapon or shield by turning aside or controlling an attack.
skills.contextNotes: Core defense; includes shield use in base model.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Brawling
skills.shortDescription: Fight effectively with punches, kicks, grapples, and rough unarmed violence.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: 1-h edged
skills.shortDescription: Fight with one-handed bladed weapons.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: 1-h conc./axe
skills.shortDescription: Fight with one-handed crushing or chopping weapons such as maces and axes.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: 2-h edged
skills.shortDescription: Fight with large two-handed bladed weapons using strength, reach, and committed technique.
skills.contextNotes: Heavy cutting or slashing weapon family. Split from the former broad 2-handed Weapons row to align better with the source combat structure.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: Polearms
skills.shortDescription: Fight with spears and longer pole weapons used at reach.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: 2-h conc./axe
skills.shortDescription: Fight with large two-handed crushing or chopping weapons such as great axes, hammers, or mauls.
skills.contextNotes: Heavy impact/chopping weapon family. Split from the former broad 2-handed Weapons row to align better with the source combat structure.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: Lance
skills.shortDescription: Fight with lance-type weapons, especially in mounted or reach-based military use.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: Throwing
skills.shortDescription: Attack accurately with thrown weapons such as javelins, knives, or axes.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Sling
skills.shortDescription: Use sling-based missile weapons effectively in combat or hunting.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Bow
skills.shortDescription: Use ordinary bows effectively in combat or hunting.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Longbow
skills.shortDescription: Use powerful longbows requiring strength, discipline, and trained missile technique.
skills.contextNotes: More specialized than ordinary bow use.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Crossbow
skills.shortDescription: Use mechanically spanned missile weapons effectively in combat.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Dex / Dex.

skills.name: Combat Experience
skills.shortDescription: Stay functional and rational under lethal danger and battlefield shock.
skills.contextNotes: Veteran-facing secondary; not baseline novice training.

skills.name: Battlefield Awareness
skills.shortDescription: Read immediate battlefield danger, openings, movement, and changing threats under pressure.

skills.name: Formation Fighting
skills.shortDescription: Hold place and fight coherently within a rank, line, shield wall, or drilled group.

skills.name: Mounted Combat
skills.shortDescription: Fight effectively while mounted and control attack timing, position, and weapon use in motion.

skills.name: Tactics
skills.shortDescription: Understand battlefield positioning, timing, coordination, and practical combat plans.
skills.contextNotes: Officer and planner skill distinct from direct troop-control.

skills.name: Captaincy
skills.shortDescription: Lead fighters, maintain order, and get people to act coherently under pressure.
skills.contextNotes: Practical troop-control and morale skill; distinct from Tactics.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Social skill; source stats Pow / Pow.

skills.name: Fencing
skills.shortDescription: Fight with fast refined civilian or dueling blades such as rapier and related weapons.
skills.notes: Themistogenes Skills sheet: Combat skill; source stats Str / Dex; note: 'Use either stat'.

skills.name: Sword & Buckler / Main-Gauche Style
skills.shortDescription: Fight using a main blade with an active off-hand defensive or companion weapon.

skills.name: Gladiatorial Style
skills.shortDescription: Fight within a specialized weapon-and-style tradition designed for formal duel or arena contexts.
skills.contextNotes: Placeholder family for later gladiatorial branches.
skills.notes: Parent attack skill depends on branch.

skills.name: Regional Martial Style
skills.shortDescription: Apply a named regional or school-based martial tradition using an existing weapon family or unarmed parent.
skills.contextNotes: Placeholder family for later Japanese, Chinese, ninja, samurai, and similar traditions.
skills.notes: Parent skill varies by branch.

professionFamilies.name: Scholar / Scribe
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Literate professional trained in records, texts, and formal learning.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Broad literate profession of archives, schools, courts, and temples.

professionFamilies.name: Merchant / Trader
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Commercial professional trained in exchange, valuation, and organized trade.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Connects goods, routes, buyers, and records.

professionFamilies.name: Courtier / Diplomat
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Elite social and political professional trained in ceremony, rank, and influence.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Operates where status, presentation, and political judgment matter.

professionFamilies.name: Healer
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Caregiver trained in immediate care, treatment, remedies, and later advanced medical branches.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Covers folk, temple, learned, and court healing subtypes.

professionFamilies.name: Engineer / Builder
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Technical professional trained in design, measurement, and practical works.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Shapes roads, structures, estates, fortifications, and major projects.

professionFamilies.name: Sailor / Navigator
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Maritime professional trained in crew work, vessel handling, and water travel.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Exists across many coastal and riverine cultures, but becomes more specialized as navigation and trade grow.

professionFamilies.name: Herdsman / Rider
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Animal-working professional trained in care, movement, and practical use of animals.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Covers pastoral, mounted, transport, and scouting roles.

professionFamilies.name: Thief / Infiltrator
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Covert operator trained in stealth, entry, concealment, and infiltration.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Covers burglary, spycraft, field stealth, and other covert roles.

professionFamilies.name: Performer
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Expressive social professional trained in music, movement, narrative, or formal presentation.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Performers exist almost everywhere, but subtype and social role vary strongly by culture, setting, and audience.

professionFamilies.name: Soldier
professionFamilies.shortDescription: Martial professional trained for armed combat, defense, and battlefield survival.
professionFamilies.contextNotes: Broad military family; subtype varies strongly. Real melee training normally includes attack skill plus Dodge and Parry.

professionSubtypes.name: Temple Scribe
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Sacred record-keeper trained in doctrine, texts, and ritual learning.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Preserves doctrine and manages sacred records.

professionSubtypes.name: Court Scribe / Clerk
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Office-trained writer and record-keeper serving a ruler, court, or bureaucracy.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Handles petitions, decrees, correspondence, and official records.

professionSubtypes.name: Local Trader
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Market trader dealing in ordinary goods, prices, and practical exchange.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Most common merchant subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Household Courtier
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Court insider skilled in favor, ceremony, and household politics.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Thrives in noble households and princely courts.

professionSubtypes.name: Folk Healer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Practical local healer using herbs, urgent care, and traditional knowledge.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Belongs to village, clan, and low-literacy settings.

professionSubtypes.name: Builder / Master Mason
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Construction specialist focused on durable works and physical building.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Emphasizes stone, timber, layout, and practical site work.

professionSubtypes.name: Deck Sailor
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Working crewman focused on routine shipboard labor and seamanship.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Baseline sailor.

professionSubtypes.name: Herdsman
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Rural stock-worker managing grazing animals and everyday animal care.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Clearest pastoral subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Burglar
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Technical intruder focused on entry, locks, traps, and hidden access.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Classic controlled-entry thief.

professionSubtypes.name: Folk Performer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Public entertainer using broad expressive skills for common audiences.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Oldest and widest form of the profession.

professionSubtypes.name: Tribal Warrior
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Clan or tribal fighter shaped by raid, hunt, and direct violence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Best for low-organization war cultures where fieldcraft and personal combat matter more than drill.

professionSubtypes.name: Levy Infantry
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Common soldier with basic organized infantry training.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Basic but coherent troop package.

professionSubtypes.name: Cavalry / Mounted Retainer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Mounted combatant using mobility and multiple mounted weapons in war.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Proper cavalry should normally know both Lance and 1-handed Edged plus Ride, Dodge, and Parry.

professionSubtypes.name: Actor
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Role-based performer using speech, presentation, and imitation for stage or social effect.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This subtype fits staged performance, civic spectacle, court entertainment, and social impersonation traditions.

professionSubtypes.name: Animal Trainer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Working-animal specialist trained to condition beasts for labor, transport, hunting, or war.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Best for roles where animals must be taught controlled tasks beyond basic care.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Assassin
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Covert killer trained in stealth, approach, and controlled lethal action.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Belongs in criminal, political, military, or clandestine settings where murder is deliberate and specialized rather than merely violent.

professionSubtypes.name: Bandit
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Predatory outlaw using ambush, intimidation, and opportunistic violence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Best for rural roads, frontier spaces, and weakly policed routes where direct theft and force matter more than urban finesse.

professionSubtypes.name: Beggar
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Street survivalist trained in reading people, soliciting support, and moving under notice without consequence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Urban margin subtype that may overlap with informants, thieves, or social survivors.

professionSubtypes.name: Bodyguard
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Protective combat specialist focused on keeping a client or patron alive in dangerous situations.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Protective martial specialist serving an individual, household, or patron.

professionSubtypes.name: Bounty Hunter
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Pursuit and capture specialist using tracking-adjacent search, intimidation, and controlled violence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Herdsman/Rider to Thief/Infiltrator because the role is primarily pursuit, coercive search, urban/frontier tracking, and controlled violence rather than animal-working.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in review pass. Family corrected to Thief/Infiltrator.

professionSubtypes.name: Bureaucrat
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Office-trained administrator handling records, routine governance, and institutional procedure.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This subtype fits literate state, temple, civic, and court systems where regular office work, records, taxation, and controlled procedure matter more than broad scholarly learning.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in review pass. Office/civil cluster.

professionSubtypes.name: Caravan Guard
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Escort fighter responsible for caravan security, route defense, and protective travel combat.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Escort and route-security martial subtype for caravans and overland trade.

professionSubtypes.name: Cavalry
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Mounted fighting subtype trained in mobility, lance use, and mounted battlefield action.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Proper cavalry should normally know both lance and sidearm use, plus ride, dodge, and parry. This is the broad mounted-war subtype rather than a noble-specific retainer branch.

professionSubtypes.name: Champion
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Elite personal combat specialist known for superior martial skill and reputation.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Fits dueling, elite retainer, household, arena, or heroic combat cultures.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Elite martial subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Chariot Driver
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Vehicle-and-team specialist skilled in driving animal-drawn war or transport chariots.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Mounted/vehicle subtype for societies where chariot combat or prestige transport is relevant.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Clan Warriors
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Kin-based martial subtype trained through collective warfare, raid culture, and clan obligation.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Close to tribal warrior but with stronger social identity as a communal fighting body.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Conman
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Fraud specialist using lies, confidence, and social manipulation for gain.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Urban and commercial criminal subtype centered on persuasion and deception rather than entry or force.

professionSubtypes.name: Courtesan
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Elite social specialist using attraction, conversation, and status-sensitive influence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Belongs in courts, elite cities, and patronage cultures where emotional reading and social access can be professionalized.

professionSubtypes.name: Crafter
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: General artisan subtype covering trained making, repair, and workshop practice.

professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Dancer/Acrobat

professionSubtypes.name: Embalmer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Specialist in body preparation, preservation, and funerary treatment.

professionSubtypes.name: Entertainer

professionSubtypes.name: Farmer

professionSubtypes.name: Fisher

professionSubtypes.name: Fixer

professionSubtypes.name: Heavy Infantry
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Disciplined close-combat infantry trained for line fighting, defense, and weight of arms.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Emphasizes sustained melee performance, cohesion, and battlefield durability.

professionSubtypes.name: Herald

professionSubtypes.name: Hermit

professionSubtypes.name: Homemaker

professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Institutional security subtype that fits prisons, castles, barracks, civic jails, or lordly compounds.

professionSubtypes.name: Lawyer

professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Belongs in civic, court, bureaucratic, and urban societies where law is a professional practice rather than customary memory alone.

professionSubtypes.name: Light Infantry

professionSubtypes.name: Master Craftsmen

professionSubtypes.name: Merchant

professionSubtypes.name: Messenger

professionSubtypes.name: Military Officer

professionSubtypes.name: Miner

professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Heavy practical subtype tied to labor, geology-by-experience, and dangerous extraction work.

professionSubtypes.name: Mourner
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Ritual or professional funeral participant trained in mourning forms, lament, and death-related observance.

professionSubtypes.name: Musician
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Instrumental performance specialist trained in music, rhythm, and public or court presentation.

professionSubtypes.name: Noble

professionSubtypes.name: Outrider/Scout

professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

professionSubtypes.name: Peddler
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Small-scale itinerant trader selling goods directly across settlements or roads.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Low-threshold commercial subtype dependent on mobility and practical bargaining rather than major capital.

professionSubtypes.name: Personal Servant
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Trusted domestic attendant trained in household service, discretion, and elite etiquette.

professionSubtypes.name: Philosopher

professionSubtypes.name: Pickpocket

professionSubtypes.name: Politician
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Power-seeking public or court actor trained in faction, rhetoric, and practical influence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Belongs in civic, courtly, and factional settings where persuasion, intrigue, and relationship management are professionalized.

professionSubtypes.name: Priest
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Sacred specialist trained in doctrine, ritual interpretation, and organized religious practice.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This is the formal religious office-holder rather than the broader folk-healer or shamanic type. It belongs especially in temple and literate sacred cultures.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Formal sacred-office subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Prospector
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Searcher for useful deposits, hidden resources, or economically valuable terrain.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Field subtype sitting between miner, scout, and practical searcher.

professionSubtypes.name: Prostitute
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Prostitute source-listed occupation subtype.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Source-listed occupation retained in the pack and awaiting deeper rule-detail refinement if needed.

professionSubtypes.name: Prostitute – Courtesan
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Sexual-social specialist whose role ranges from survival trade to elite influence depending context.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Handled here as the courtly/high-social end of the source label; lower-end prostitution can be modeled more simply in play.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Sailor
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: General seafaring worker trained in ordinary shipboard labor, vessel routine, and waterborne travel.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This is the generic sailor subtype beneath more specialized navigators and officers. It fits commercial, military, and fishing vessels.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Generic maritime subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Scribe
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: General literate record-keeper trained in copying, records, and formal writing.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Broad scribal subtype suitable where a more specific temple or court clerk package is not needed.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Shaman
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Ritual specialist blending healing, spirit-work, omen reading, and communal guidance.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Best for low-literacy, clan, pastoral, or orally transmitted sacred traditions rather than formal temple bureaucracy.

professionSubtypes.name: Ships Officer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Command-capable maritime subtype overseeing crew work, route discipline, and shipboard order.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Bridges working seamanship and leadership rather than full independent navigation alone.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Slave master
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Harsh supervisory specialist responsible for coerced labor, discipline, and control of dependents.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Authority-and-control subtype for coercive labor systems; placed here because the role is primarily managerial and social-authoritative rather than covert.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in review pass. Family corrected in civic-control direction.

professionSubtypes.name: Smuggler
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Illicit trader moving goods across borders, tolls, law, or social restrictions.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Commercial-criminal subtype balancing trade skill with concealment and route knowledge.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Illicit trade subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Soothsayer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Divinatory specialist focused on signs, omens, and prophetic interpretation.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Sacred or mystical subtype centered more on foretelling and interpretation than on institutional priestly office.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass.

professionSubtypes.name: Spy
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Covert political operator using disguise, stealth, and controlled social deception.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This subtype belongs in court, state, military, and intrigue-heavy settings where infiltration is at least as social as it is physical.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Covert-political subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Street Thug
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Urban intimidator or enforcer using threat, violence, and local underworld presence.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Criminal muscle subtype for city and market environments.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Urban enforcer subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Student
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Formal learner in a literate, scholarly, or institutional training environment.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Broad educational subtype representing someone in active study rather than a fully established profession.
professionSubtypes.notes: Completed package in final completion pass. Educational subtype.

professionSubtypes.name: Tax Collector
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Revenue official trained to assess, record, and extract dues, rents, or taxes.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Administrative subtype for literate states, temples, estates, and civic authorities where revenue collection is formalized.

professionSubtypes.name: Thief
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: General covert criminal operator trained in stealth, entry, concealment, and theft.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: This is the broad rogue subtype, suitable where a more specific burglar, pickpocket, or spy package is not needed.

professionSubtypes.name: Torturer
professionSubtypes.shortDescription: Coercive interrogator or punitive specialist using intimidation, pain, and confinement knowledge.
professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Merchant/Trader to Thief/Infiltrator because the role fits coercive interrogation, prison, and intimidation functions rather than commerce.

professionSubtypes.name: Watchman

professionSubtypes.contextNotes: Source-listed occupation. Reassigned from Thief/Infiltrator to Soldier because the role is primarily guard, patrol, and low-grade civic security rather than covert intrusion.

professionSubtypes.name: Woodcutter
